description: real benchmark extraction for feat(uptime): Add ability to use queues to manage parallelism

vars.prTitle: feat(uptime): Add ability to use queues to manage parallelism
vars.prBody:
One potential problem we have with batch processing is that any one slow item will clog up the whole batch. This pr implements a queueing method instead, where we keep N queues that each have their own workers. There's still a chance of individual items backlogging a queue, but we can try increased concurrency here to reduce the chances of that happening

<!-- Describe your PR here. -->
vars.changedFiles:
[{"filename":"src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py","patchWithLinesStr":"## file: 'src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py'

@@ -0,0 +1,345 @@
__new hunk__
1 +from __future__ import annotations
2 +
3 +import logging
4 +import queue
5 +import threading
6 +import time
7 +from collections import defaultdict
8 +from collections.abc import Callable
9 +from dataclasses import dataclass
10 +from typing import Any, Generic, TypeVar
11 +
12 +import sentry_sdk
13 +from arroyo.backends.kafka.consumer import KafkaPayload
14 +from arroyo.processing.strategies import ProcessingStrategy
15 +from arroyo.types import BrokerValue, FilteredPayload, Message, Partition
16 +
17 +from sentry.utils import metrics
18 +
19 +logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20 +
21 +T = TypeVar("T")
22 +
23 +
24 +@dataclass
25 +class WorkItem(Generic[T]):
26 +    """Work item that includes the original message for offset tracking."""
27 +
28 +    partition: Partition
29 +    offset: int
30 +    result: T
31 +    message: Message[KafkaPayload | FilteredPayload]
32 +
33 +
34 +class OffsetTracker:
35 +    """
36 +    Tracks outstanding offsets and determines which offsets are safe to commit.
37 +
38 +    - Tracks offsets per partition
39 +    - Only commits offsets when all prior offsets are processed
40 +    - Thread-safe for concurrent access with per-partition locks
41 +    """
42 +
43 +    def __init__(self) -> None:
44 +        self.all_offsets: dict[Partition, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
45 +        self.outstanding: dict[Partition, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
46 +        self.last_committed: dict[Partition, int] = {}
47 +        self.partition_locks: dict[Partition, threading.Lock] = {}
48 +
49 +    def _get_partition_lock(self, partition: Partition) -> threading.Lock:
50 +        """Get or create a lock for a partition."""
51 +        lock = self.partition_locks.get(partition)
52 +        if lock:
53 +            return lock
54 +        return self.partition_locks.setdefault(partition, threading.Lock())
55 +
56 +    def add_offset(self, partition: Partition, offset: int) -> None:
57 +        """Record that we've started processing an offset."""
58 +        with self._get_partition_lock(partition):
59 +            self.all_offsets[partition].add(offset)
60 +            self.outstanding[partition].add(offset)
61 +
62 +    def complete_offset(self, partition: Partition, offset: int) -> None:
63 +        """Mark an offset as completed."""
64 +        with self._get_partition_lock(partition):
65 +            self.outstanding[partition].discard(offset)
66 +
67 +    def get_committable_offsets(self) -> dict[Partition, int]:
68 +        """
69 +        Get the highest offset per partition that can be safely committed.
70 +
71 +        For each partition, finds the highest contiguous offset that has been processed.
72 +        """
73 +        committable = {}
74 +        for partition in list(self.all_offsets.keys()):
75 +            with self._get_partition_lock(partition):
76 +                all_offsets = self.all_offsets[partition]
77 +                if not all_offsets:
78 +                    continue
79 +
80 +                outstanding = self.outstanding[partition]
81 +                last_committed = self.last_committed.get(partition, -1)
82 +
83 +                min_offset = min(all_offsets)
84 +                max_offset = max(all_offsets)
85 +
86 +                start = max(last_committed + 1, min_offset)
87 +
88 +                highest_committable = last_committed
89 +                for offset in range(start, max_offset + 1):
90 +                    if offset in all_offsets and offset not in outstanding:
91 +                        highest_committable = offset
92 +                    else:
93 +                        break
94 +
95 +                if highest_committable > last_committed:
96 +                    committable[partition] = highest_committable
97 +
98 +        return committable
99 +
100 +    def mark_committed(self, partition: Partition, offset: int) -> None:
101 +        """Update the last committed offset for a partition."""
102 +        with self._get_partition_lock(partition):
103 +            self.last_committed[partition] = offset
104 +            # Remove all offsets <= committed offset
105 +            self.all_offsets[partition] = {o for o in self.all_offsets[partition] if o > offset}
106 +
107 +
108 +class OrderedQueueWorker(threading.Thread, Generic[T]):
109 +    """Worker thread that processes items from a queue in order."""
110 +
111 +    def __init__(
112 +        self,
113 +        worker_id: int,
114 +        work_queue: queue.Queue[WorkItem[T]],
115 +        result_processor: Callable[[str, T], None],
116 +        identifier: str,
117 +        offset_tracker: OffsetTracker,
118 +    ) -> None:
119 +        super().__init__(daemon=True)
120 +        self.worker_id = worker_id
121 +        self.work_queue = work_queue
122 +        self.result_processor = result_processor
123 +        self.identifier = identifier
124 +        self.offset_tracker = offset_tracker
125 +        self.shutdown = False
126 +
127 +    def run(self) -> None:
128 +        """Process items from the queue in order."""
129 +        while not self.shutdown:
130 +            try:
131 +                work_item = self.work_queue.get()
132 +            except queue.ShutDown:
133 +                break
134 +
135 +            try:
136 +                with sentry_sdk.start_transaction(
137 +                    op="queue_worker.process",
138 +                    name=f"monitors.{self.identifier}.worker_{self.worker_id}",
139 +                ):
140 +                    self.result_processor(self.identifier, work_item.result)
141 +
142 +            except queue.ShutDown:
143 +                break
144 +            except Exception:
145 +                logger.exception(
146 +                    "Unexpected error in queue worker", extra={"worker_id": self.worker_id}
147 +                )
148 +            finally:
149 +                self.offset_tracker.complete_offset(work_item.partition, work_item.offset)
150 +                metrics.gauge(
151 +                    "remote_subscriptions.queue_worker.queue_depth",
152 +                    self.work_queue.qsize(),
153 +                    tags={
154 +                        "identifier": self.identifier,
155 +                    },
156 +                )
157 +
158 +
159 +class FixedQueuePool(Generic[T]):
160 +    """
161 +    Fixed pool of queues that guarantees order within groups.
162 +
163 +    Key properties:
164 +    - Each group is consistently assigned to the same queue
165 +    - Each queue has exactly one worker thread
166 +    - Items within a queue are processed in FIFO order
167 +    - No dynamic reassignment that could break ordering
168 +    - Tracks offset completion for safe commits
169 +    """
170 +
171 +    def __init__(
172 +        self,
173 +        result_processor: Callable[[str, T], None],
174 +        identifier: str,
175 +        num_queues: int = 20,
176 +    ) -> None:
177 +        self.result_processor = result_processor
178 +        self.identifier = identifier
179 +        self.num_queues = num_queues
180 +        self.offset_tracker = OffsetTracker()
181 +        self.queues: list[queue.Queue[WorkItem[T]]] = []
182 +        self.workers: list[OrderedQueueWorker[T]] = []
183 +
184 +        for i in range(num_queues):
185 +            work_queue: queue.Queue[WorkItem[T]] = queue.Queue()
186 +            self.queues.append(work_queue)
187 +
188 +            worker = OrderedQueueWorker[T](
189 +                worker_id=i,
190 +                work_queue=work_queue,
191 +                result_processor=result_processor,
192 +                identifier=identifier,
193 +                offset_tracker=self.offset_tracker,
194 +            )
195 +            worker.start()
196 +            self.workers.append(worker)
197 +
198 +    def get_queue_for_group(self, group_key: str) -> int:
199 +        """
200 +        Get queue index for a group using consistent hashing.
201 +        """
202 +        return hash(group_key) % self.num_queues
203 +
204 +    def submit(self, group_key: str, work_item: WorkItem[T]) -> None:
205 +        """
206 +        Submit a work item to the appropriate queue.
207 +        """
208 +        queue_index = self.get_queue_for_group(group_key)
209 +        work_queue = self.queues[queue_index]
210 +
211 +        self.offset_tracker.add_offset(work_item.partition, work_item.offset)
212 +        work_queue.put(work_item)
213 +
214 +    def get_stats(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
215 +        """Get statistics about queue depths."""
216 +        queue_depths = [q.qsize() for q in self.queues]
217 +        return {
218 +            "queue_depths": queue_depths,
219 +            "total_items": sum(queue_depths),
220 +        }
221 +
222 +    def wait_until_empty(self, timeout: float = 5.0) -> bool:
223 +        """Wait until all queues are empty. Returns True if successful, False if timeout."""
224 +        start_time = time.time()
225 +        while time.time() - start_time < timeout:
226 +            if self.get_stats()["total_items"] == 0:
227 +                return True
228 +            time.sleep(0.01)
229 +        return False
230 +
231 +    def shutdown(self) -> None:
232 +        """Gracefully shutdown all workers."""
233 +        for worker in self.workers:
234 +            worker.shutdown = True
235 +
236 +        for q in self.queues:
237 +            try:
238 +                q.shutdown(immediate=False)
239 +            except Exception:
240 +                logger.exception("Error shutting down queue")
241 +
242 +        for worker in self.workers:
243 +            worker.join(timeout=5.0)
244 +
245 +
246 +class SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy(ProcessingStrategy[KafkaPayload], Generic[T]):
247 +    """
248 +    Processing strategy that uses a fixed pool of queues.
249 +
250 +    Guarantees:
251 +    - Items for the same group are processed in order
252 +    - No item is lost or processed out of order
253 +    - Natural backpressure when queues fill up
254 +    - Only commits offsets after successful processing
255 +    """
256 +
257 +    def __init__(
258 +        self,
259 +        queue_pool: FixedQueuePool[T],
260 +        decoder: Callable[[KafkaPayload | FilteredPayload], T | None],
261 +        grouping_fn: Callable[[T], str],
262 +        commit_function: Callable[[dict[Partition, int]], None],
263 +    ) -> None:
264 +        self.queue_pool = queue_pool
265 +        self.decoder = decoder
266 +        self.grouping_fn = grouping_fn
267 +        self.commit_function = commit_function
268 +        self.shutdown_event = threading.Event()
269 +
270 +        self.commit_thread = threading.Thread(target=self._commit_loop, daemon=True)
271 +        self.commit_thread.start()
272 +
273 +    def _commit_loop(self) -> None:
274 +        while not self.shutdown_event.is_set():
275 +            try:
276 +                self.shutdown_event.wait(1.0)
277 +
278 +                committable = self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.get_committable_offsets()
279 +
280 +                if committable:
281 +                    metrics.incr(
282 +                        "remote_subscriptions.queue_pool.offsets_committed",
283 +                        len(committable),
284 +                        tags={"identifier": self.queue_pool.identifier},
285 +                    )
286 +
287 +                    self.commit_function(committable)
288 +                    for partition, offset in committable.items():
289 +                        self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.mark_committed(partition, offset)
290 +            except Exception:
291 +                logger.exception("Error in commit loop")
292 +
293 +    def submit(self, message: Message[KafkaPayload | FilteredPayload]) -> None:
294 +        try:
295 +            result = self.decoder(message.payload)
296 +
297 +            assert isinstance(message.value, BrokerValue)
298 +            partition = message.value.partition
299 +            offset = message.value.offset
300 +
301 +            if result is None:
302 +                self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.add_offset(partition, offset)
303 +                self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.complete_offset(partition, offset)
304 +                return
305 +
306 +            group_key = self.grouping_fn(result)
307 +
308 +            work_item = WorkItem(
309 +                partition=partition,
310 +                offset=offset,
311 +                result=result,
312 +                message=message,
313 +            )
314 +
315 +            self.queue_pool.submit(group_key, work_item)
316 +
317 +        except Exception:
318 +            logger.exception("Error submitting message to queue")
319 +            if isinstance(message.value, BrokerValue):
320 +                self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.add_offset(
321 +                    message.value.partition, message.value.offset
322 +                )
323 +                self.queue_pool.offset_tracker.complete_offset(
324 +                    message.value.partition, message.value.offset
325 +                )
326 +
327 +    def poll(self) -> None:
328 +        stats = self.queue_pool.get_stats()
329 +        metrics.gauge(
330 +            "remote_subscriptions.queue_pool.total_queued",
331 +            stats["total_items"],
332 +            tags={"identifier": self.queue_pool.identifier},
333 +        )
334 +
335 +    def close(self) -> None:
336 +        self.shutdown_event.set()
337 +        self.commit_thread.join(timeout=5.0)
338 +        self.queue_pool.shutdown()
339 +
340 +    def terminate(self) -> None:
341 +        self.shutdown_event.set()
342 +        self.queue_pool.shutdown()
343 +
344 +    def join(self, timeout: float | None = None) -> None:
345 +        self.close()"},{"filename":"src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/result_consumer.py","patchWithLinesStr":"## file: 'src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/result_consumer.py'

@@ -0,0 +1,421 @@
__new hunk__
1 +"""
2 +Tests for the thread-queue-parallel result consumer implementation.
3 +"""
4 +
5 +import threading
6 +from datetime import datetime
7 +from typing import Any
8 +from unittest import mock
9 +
10 +from arroyo.backends.kafka import KafkaPayload
11 +from arroyo.types import BrokerValue, FilteredPayload, Message, Partition, Topic
12 +
13 +from sentry.remote_subscriptions.consumers.queue_consumer import (
14 +    FixedQueuePool,
15 +    OffsetTracker,
16 +    SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy,
17 +    WorkItem,
18 +)
19 +from sentry.testutils.cases import TestCase
20 +
21 +
22 +class TestOffsetTracker(TestCase):
23 +    def setUp(self):
24 +        self.tracker = OffsetTracker()
25 +        self.partition1 = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
26 +        self.partition2 = Partition(Topic("test"), 1)
27 +
28 +    def test_simple_tracking(self):
29 +        """Test basic offset tracking and committing."""
30 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition1, 100)
31 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition1, 101)
32 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition1, 102)
33 +
34 +        committable = self.tracker.get_committable_offsets()
35 +        assert committable == {}
36 +
37 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition1, 100)
38 +        committable = self.tracker.get_committable_offsets()
39 +        assert committable == {self.partition1: 100}
40 +
41 +        self.tracker.mark_committed(self.partition1, 100)
42 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition1, 102)
43 +        committable = self.tracker.get_committable_offsets()
44 +        assert committable == {}
45 +
46 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition1, 101)
47 +        committable = self.tracker.get_committable_offsets()
48 +        assert committable == {self.partition1: 102}
49 +
50 +    def test_multiple_partitions(self):
51 +        """Test tracking across multiple partitions."""
52 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition1, 100)
53 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition1, 101)
54 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition2, 200)
55 +        self.tracker.add_offset(self.partition2, 201)
56 +
57 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition1, 100)
58 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition2, 200)
59 +        self.tracker.complete_offset(self.partition2, 201)
60 +
61 +        committable = self.tracker.get_committable_offsets()
62 +        assert committable == {self.partition1: 100, self.partition2: 201}
63 +
64 +
65 +class TestFixedQueuePool(TestCase):
66 +    def setUp(self):
67 +        self.processed_items: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
68 +        self.process_lock = threading.Lock()
69 +        self.process_complete_event = threading.Event()
70 +        self.items_processed = 0
71 +        self.expected_items = 0
72 +
73 +        def result_processor(identifier: str, item: str):
74 +            with self.process_lock:
75 +                self.processed_items.append((identifier, item))
76 +                self.items_processed += 1
77 +                if self.items_processed >= self.expected_items:
78 +                    self.process_complete_event.set()
79 +
80 +        self.pool = FixedQueuePool(
81 +            result_processor=result_processor,
82 +            identifier="test",
83 +            num_queues=3,
84 +        )
85 +
86 +    def tearDown(self):
87 +        self.pool.shutdown()
88 +
89 +    def test_consistent_group_assignment(self):
90 +        """Test that groups are consistently assigned to the same queue."""
91 +        group_key = "group1"
92 +        queue_index1 = self.pool.get_queue_for_group(group_key)
93 +        queue_index2 = self.pool.get_queue_for_group(group_key)
94 +        queue_index3 = self.pool.get_queue_for_group(group_key)
95 +
96 +        assert queue_index1 == queue_index2 == queue_index3
97 +
98 +    def test_different_groups_distributed(self):
99 +        """Test that different groups are distributed across queues."""
100 +        queue_indices = set()
101 +        for i in range(20):
102 +            group_key = f"group{i}"
103 +            queue_index = self.pool.get_queue_for_group(group_key)
104 +            queue_indices.add(queue_index)
105 +
106 +        assert len(queue_indices) == 3
107 +
108 +    def test_ordered_processing_within_group(self):
109 +        """Test that items within a group are processed in order."""
110 +        partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
111 +        group_key = "ordered_group"
112 +
113 +        self.expected_items = 5
114 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()

116 +        for i in range(5):

130 +            self.pool.submit(group_key, work_item)

137 +    def test_concurrent_processing_across_groups(self):
138 +        """Test that different groups are processed concurrently."""
139 +        partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
140 +
141 +        self.expected_items = 6

143 +
144 +        for i in range(6):

147 +                partition=partition,

156 +                    )
157 +                ),

160 +

162 +        assert len(self.processed_items) == 6
163 +
164 +        groups_seen = set()

166 +            if item.startswith("item_group_"):
167 +                # Extract the group number (0, 1, or 2)

170 +                    group_num = parts[2]
171 +                    groups_seen.add(group_num)
172 +
173 +        assert len(groups_seen) == 3

209 +class TestSimpleQueueProcessingStrategy(TestCase):
210 +    def setUp(self):
211 +        self.processed_results: list[Any] = []
212 +        self.committed_offsets: dict[Partition, int] = {}
213 +        self.process_lock = threading.Lock()
214 +        self.process_complete_event = threading.Event()
215 +        self.commit_event = threading.Event()
216 +        self.items_processed = 0
217 +        self.expected_items = 0
218 +
219 +        def result_processor(identifier: str, result: dict):
220 +            with self.process_lock:
221 +                self.processed_results.append(result)
222 +                self.items_processed += 1
223 +                if self.items_processed >= self.expected_items:
224 +                    self.process_complete_event.set()
225 +
226 +        self.queue_pool = FixedQueuePool(
227 +            result_processor=result_processor,
228 +            identifier="test",
229 +            num_queues=2,
230 +        )
231 +
232 +        def commit_function(offsets: dict[Partition, int]):
233 +            with self.process_lock:
234 +                self.committed_offsets.update(offsets)
235 +                self.commit_event.set()
236 +
237 +        def decoder(payload: KafkaPayload | FilteredPayload) -> dict | None:
238 +            if isinstance(payload, FilteredPayload):
239 +                return None
240 +            return {"subscription_id": payload.value.decode(), "data": "test"}
241 +
242 +        def grouping_fn(result: dict) -> str:
243 +            return result["subscription_id"]
244 +
245 +        self.strategy = SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy(
246 +            queue_pool=self.queue_pool,
247 +            decoder=decoder,
248 +            grouping_fn=grouping_fn,
249 +            commit_function=commit_function,
250 +        )
251 +
252 +    def tearDown(self):
253 +        self.strategy.close()
254 +
255 +    def create_message(self, subscription_id: str, partition: int, offset: int) -> Message:
256 +        """Helper to create a test message."""
257 +        payload = KafkaPayload(
258 +            key=None,
259 +            value=subscription_id.encode(),
260 +            headers=[],
261 +        )
262 +        return Message(
263 +            BrokerValue(
264 +                payload,
265 +                Partition(Topic("test"), partition),
266 +                offset,
267 +                datetime.now(),
268 +            )
269 +        )
270 +
271 +    def test_message_processing(self):
272 +        """Test basic message processing."""
273 +        partition = 0
274 +        message = self.create_message("sub1", partition, 100)
275 +
276 +        self.expected_items = 1
277 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
278 +
279 +        self.strategy.submit(message)
280 +
281 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
282 +        assert len(self.processed_results) == 1
283 +        assert self.processed_results[0]["subscription_id"] == "sub1"
284 +
285 +    def test_offset_committing(self):
286 +        """Test that offsets are committed after processing."""
287 +        partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
288 +
289 +        self.expected_items = 5
290 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
291 +        self.commit_event.clear()
292 +        for i in range(5):
293 +            message = self.create_message("sub1", 0, 100 + i)
294 +            self.strategy.submit(message)
295 +
296 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
297 +        assert self.commit_event.wait(timeout=2.0), "Commit did not happen in time"
298 +
299 +        assert partition in self.committed_offsets
300 +        assert self.committed_offsets[partition] == 104
301 +
302 +    def test_preserves_order_within_group(self):
303 +        """Test that messages for the same subscription are processed in order."""
304 +        self.expected_items = 5
305 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
306 +
307 +        for i in range(5):
308 +            message = self.create_message("sub1", 0, 100 + i)
309 +            self.strategy.submit(message)
310 +
311 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
312 +        assert len(self.processed_results) == 5
313 +
314 +    def test_concurrent_processing_different_groups(self):
315 +        """Test that different subscriptions are processed concurrently."""
316 +        self.expected_items = 4
317 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
318 +
319 +        for i in range(4):
320 +            message = self.create_message(f"sub{i % 2}", 0, 100 + i)
321 +            self.strategy.submit(message)
322 +
323 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
324 +
325 +        assert len(self.processed_results) == 4
326 +
327 +    def test_handles_invalid_messages(self):
328 +        """Test that invalid messages don't block offset commits."""
329 +        partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
330 +
331 +        self.expected_items = 1
332 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
333 +        self.commit_event.clear()
334 +
335 +        invalid_message = Message(
336 +            BrokerValue(
337 +                FilteredPayload(),
338 +                partition,
339 +                100,
340 +                datetime.now(),
341 +            )
342 +        )
343 +
344 +        self.strategy.submit(invalid_message)
345 +        self.strategy.submit(self.create_message("sub1", 0, 101))
346 +
347 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
348 +        assert self.commit_event.wait(timeout=2.0), "Commit did not happen in time"
349 +        assert self.committed_offsets.get(partition) == 101
350 +
351 +    def test_offset_gaps_block_commits(self):
352 +        """Test that gaps in offsets prevent committing past the gap."""
353 +        partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 0)
354 +
355 +        self.expected_items = 3  # First batch
356 +        self.process_complete_event.clear()
357 +        self.commit_event.clear()
358 +
359 +        self.strategy.submit(self.create_message("sub1", 0, 100))
360 +        self.strategy.submit(self.create_message("sub1", 0, 102))
361 +        self.strategy.submit(self.create_message("sub1", 0, 103))
362 +
363 +        assert self.process_complete_event.wait(timeout=5.0), "Processing did not complete in time"
364 +        assert self.commit_event.wait(timeout=2.0), "Commit did not happen in time"
365 +        assert self.committed_offsets.get(partition) == 100
366 +
367 +        self.expected_items = 4
368 +        self.commit_event.clear()
369 +        self.strategy.submit(self.create_message("sub1", 0, 101))
370 +
371 +        assert self.commit_event.wait(timeout=2.0), "Second commit did not happen in time"
372 +        assert self.committed_offsets.get(partition) == 103
373 +

1926 +    def test_thread_queue_parallel_offset_gaps(self) -> None:
1927 +        """
1928 +        Test that offset gaps prevent committing past the gap.
1929 +        """
1930 +        all_commits = []
1931 +
1932 +        def track_commits(offsets: Mapping[Partition, int], force: bool = False) -> None:
1933 +            all_commits.append(dict(offsets))
1934 +
1935 +        factory = UptimeResultsStrategyFactory(
1936 +            mode="thread-queue-parallel",
1937 +            max_workers=1,
1938 +        )
1939 +
1940 +        test_partition = Partition(Topic("test"), 1)
1941 +        consumer = factory.create_with_partitions(track_commits, {test_partition: 0})
1942 +
1943 +        with mock.patch.object(type(factory.result_processor), "__call__"):
1944 +            codec = kafka_definition.get_topic_codec(kafka_definition.Topic.UPTIME_RESULTS)
1945 +            for offset in [100, 102, 103]:
1946 +                result = self.create_uptime_result(
1947 +                    self.subscription.subscription_id,
1948 +                    scheduled_check_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=5),
1949 +                )
1950 +                message = Message(
1951 +                    BrokerValue(
1952 +                        KafkaPayload(None, codec.encode(result), []),
1953 +                        test_partition,
1954 +                        offset,
1955 +                        datetime.now(),
1956 +                    )
1957 +                )
1958 +                consumer.submit(message)
1959 +
1960 +            queue_pool = factory.queue_pool
1961 +            max_wait = 20
1962 +            for _ in range(max_wait):
1963 +                assert queue_pool is not None
1964 +                stats = queue_pool.get_stats()
1965 +                if stats["total_items"] == 0 and len(all_commits) > 0:
1966 +                    time.sleep(0.2)
1967 +                    break
1968 +                time.sleep(0.1)
1969 +
1970 +            assert len(all_commits) > 0, "No commits happened"
1971 +
1972 +            last_commit = all_commits[-1]
1973 +            assert test_partition in last_commit
1974 +            actual_offset = last_commit[test_partition]
1975 +            assert (
1976 +                actual_offset == 101
1977 +            ), f"Expected to commit offset 101 (next to read after processing 100), but got {actual_offset}"
1978 +
1979 +            for commit in all_commits:
1980 +                if test_partition in commit:
1981 +                    assert (
1982 +                        commit[test_partition] <= 101
1983 +                    ), f"Should not commit past the gap, but got {commit[test_partition]}"
1984 +
1985 +        factory.shutdown()
1986 +
1987 +    def test_thread_queue_parallel_graceful_shutdown(self) -> None:
1988 +        """
1989 +        Test that the thread-queue-parallel consumer shuts down gracefully.
1990 +        """
1991 +        factory = UptimeResultsStrategyFactory(
1992 +            mode="thread-queue-parallel",
1993 +            max_workers=3,
1994 +        )
1995 +        consumer = factory.create_with_partitions(mock.Mock(), {self.partition: 0})
1996 +
1997 +        for i in range(5):
1998 +            result = self.create_uptime_result(
1999 +                self.subscription.subscription_id,
2000 +                scheduled_check_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=i),
2001 +            )
2002 +            self.send_result(result, consumer=consumer)
2003 +
2004 +        factory.shutdown()
2005 +        assert factory.queue_pool is None
2006 +

2008  class ProcessResultParallelTest(ProcessResultTest):
2009      strategy_processing_mode = "parallel"

2012 +class ProcessResultThreadQueueParallelKafkaTest(UptimeTestCase):
2013 +    """
2014 +    Integration test for thread-queue-parallel consumer with actual Kafka offset verification.
2015 +    """
2016 +
2017 +    pytestmark = [requires_kafka]
2018 +
2019 +    def test_thread_queue_parallel_kafka_offset_commit(self) -> None:
2020 +        """
2021 +        Test that offsets are actually committed to Kafka consumer group.
2022 +        """
2023 +        subscription = self.create_uptime_subscription(
2024 +            subscription_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, interval_seconds=300, region_slugs=["default"]
2025 +        )
2026 +        self.create_project_uptime_subscription(
2027 +            uptime_subscription=subscription,
2028 +            owner=self.user,
2029 +        )
2030 +
2031 +        test_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
2032 +        test_topic = f"uptime-test-{test_id}"
2033 +        consumer_group = f"uptime-test-group-{test_id}"
2034 +        cluster_options = get_kafka_admin_cluster_options(
2035 +            "default", {"allow.auto.create.topics": "true"}
2036 +        )
2037 +        admin_client = AdminClient(cluster_options)
2038 +        try:
2039 +            create_topics("default", [test_topic])
2040 +            wait_for_topics(admin_client, [test_topic])
2041 +
2042 +            producer_conf = settings.KAFKA_CLUSTERS["default"]["common"]
2043 +            producer = Producer(producer_conf)
2044 +
2045 +            codec = kafka_definition.get_topic_codec(kafka_definition.Topic.UPTIME_RESULTS)
2046 +            for i in range(5):
2047 +                result = self.create_uptime_result(
2048 +                    subscription.subscription_id,
2049 +                    scheduled_check_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=5 - i),
2050 +                )
2051 +                encoded = codec.encode(result)
2052 +                producer.produce(test_topic, value=encoded, partition=0)
2053 +
2054 +            producer.flush()
2055 +
2056 +            factory = UptimeResultsStrategyFactory(
2057 +                mode="thread-queue-parallel",
2058 +                max_workers=2,
2059 +            )
2060 +
2061 +            with override_settings(
2062 +                KAFKA_TOPIC_OVERRIDES={
2063 +                    "uptime-results": test_topic,
2064 +                }
2065 +            ):
2066 +                commit_count = 0
2067 +                commits_made = []
2068 +                original_create_with_partitions = factory.create_with_partitions
2069 +
2070 +                def create_with_partitions_tracking(commit, partitions):
2071 +                    def tracked_commit(
2072 +                        offsets: Mapping[Partition, int], force: bool = False
2073 +                    ) -> None:
2074 +                        nonlocal commit_count
2075 +                        commit_count += 1
2076 +                        commits_made.append(dict(offsets))
2077 +                        return commit(offsets, force)
2078 +
2079 +                    return original_create_with_partitions(tracked_commit, partitions)
2080 +
2081 +                factory.create_with_partitions = create_with_partitions_tracking  # type: ignore[method-assign]
2082 +                consumer_config = build_kafka_consumer_configuration(
2083 +                    settings.KAFKA_CLUSTERS["default"]["common"],
2084 +                    group_id=consumer_group,
2085 +                    auto_offset_reset="earliest",
2086 +                )
2087 +
2088 +                consumer = KafkaConsumer(consumer_config)
2089 +                processor = StreamProcessor(
2090 +                    consumer=consumer,
2091 +                    topic=Topic(test_topic),
2092 +                    processor_factory=factory,
2093 +                    commit_policy=ONCE_PER_SECOND,
2094 +                )
2095 +
2096 +                with mock.patch.object(
2097 +                    type(factory.result_processor), "__call__"
2098 +                ) as mock_processor:
2099 +                    mock_processor.return_value = None
2100 +
2101 +                    start_time = time.time()
2102 +                    while time.time() - start_time < 5:
2103 +                        processor._run_once()
2104 +                        time.sleep(0.1)
2105 +
2106 +                processor._shutdown()
2107 +                factory.shutdown()
2108 +
2109 +                verify_consumer = Consumer(
2110 +                    {
2111 +                        **settings.KAFKA_CLUSTERS["default"]["common"],
2112 +                        "group.id": consumer_group,
2113 +                        "auto.offset.reset": "earliest",
2114 +                        "enable.auto.commit": False,
2115 +                    }
2116 +                )
2117 +
2118 +                partitions = [TopicPartition(test_topic, 0)]
2119 +                committed = verify_consumer.committed(partitions)
2120 +                assert commit_count >= 1, f"Expected at least 1 commit, got {commit_count}"
2121 +
2122 +                if commits_made:
2123 +                    last_commit = commits_made[-1]
2124 +                    expected_partition = Partition(topic=Topic(test_topic), index=0)
2125 +                    assert (
2126 +                        expected_partition in last_commit
2127 +                    ), f"Expected partition {expected_partition} in commit"
2128 +                    assert (
2129 +                        last_commit[expected_partition] == 5
2130 +                    ), f"Expected offset 5, got {last_commit[expected_partition]}"
2131 +
2132 +                assert len(committed) == 1
2133 +                assert committed[0].topic == test_topic
2134 +                assert committed[0].partition == 0
2135 +                # We sent 5 messages (0-4), so the committed offset should be 5
2136 +                assert (
2137 +                    committed[0].offset == 5
2138 +                ), f"Expected committed offset 5, got {committed[0].offset}"
2139 +
2140 +                verify_consumer.close()
2141 +
2142 +        finally:
2143 +            try:
2144 +                admin_client.delete_topics([test_topic])
2145 +            except Exception:
2146 +                pass"}]
